title: Codepad Language Rule Set
description: Defines, for one language, everything the on-screen keyboard's Blocks panel, the symbol maker, and the module reference editor need without hardcoding per-language behavior in extension code.

$schema.description: Optional self-reference to this schema file, so editors like VS Code can validate the instance document on save.

indentation.description: Whether blocks are delimited by braces or by indentation (Python-style). All MVP languages (C, Java, Kotlin) are "brace".

label.description: Button label shown on the on-screen keyboard's Blocks panel.

template.description: Snippet body using standard VS Code snippet syntax: ${1:placeholder} tab-stops and a final $0 for the resting caret position after insertion.

modifiers.description: Pick-one-or-more modifier groups shown in the symbol maker wizard before naming, e.g. storage class or visibility.

types.description: Fixed list of types offered for this kind (e.g. C primitive types for "variable"), so the wizard stays click-only instead of requiring free type entry.

template.description: Declaration template. Supports {name} and {type} placeholders, plus one {<modifierGroupId>} placeholder per modifier group, substituted from the wizard's selections.

multiple.description: Whether more than one option in this group can be selected at once (e.g. "static" + "const").

text.description: Literal text substituted into the template's placeholder when this option is selected, e.g. "static " (including trailing space, if any).

statementTemplate.description: e.g. "#include <{path}>" or "import {path};". Must contain a {path} placeholder.
